title: Non-deterministic maker-taker assignment for concurrent orders
description: When two concurrent orders are matched, the assignment of maker vs taker roles is non-deterministic. Depending on the order of contract processing, either party could be assigned either role. This affects fee calculations, priority rules, and any logic that depends on the maker/taker distinction.
recommendation: Introduce deterministic rules for maker/taker assignment (e.g., based on order creation time, price priority, or explicit role designation).

oracle.description: Maker/taker role assignment depends on non-deterministic execution ordering rather than deterministic criteria.
oracle.static_signal: Role assignment logic that depends on the order of input parameters without explicit sorting/comparison criteria
oracle.symbolic_property: forall o1 o2: match(o1, o2).maker == match(o2, o1).maker (determinism)

notes: Relevant to any DAML matching engine. Non-determinism in DAML is a subtle issue since the ledger model is otherwise deterministic.
